name: tool-use
context: Give Claude callable functions via tool definitions — client-side tools run in your app, server-side tools run on Anthropic
info: Tool use (function calling) lets Claude invoke functions you define. You pass tool definitions with `name`, `description`, and `input_schema` (JSON Schema) in the `tools` parameter. When Claude decides to use a tool, the response contains a `tool_use` content block with `id`, `name`, and `input`. Your code executes the function and sends back a `tool_result` content block with the output. This creates an agentic loop: prompt → tool_use → execute → tool_result → continue. Client-side tools run in your application; server-side tools (web_search, code_execution, web_fetch) run on Anthropic infrastructure. The `tool_choice` parameter controls when Claude uses tools: `auto` (default, Claude decides), `any` (must use a tool), `tool` (must use a specific tool), `none` (disabled). Add `strict: true` to enforce exact schema conformance.
triggers: create a tool for claude to call
build an agentic loop with claude
add tools to a claude api request

sections.title: Defining tools
sections.body: Each tool needs `name` (lowercase, underscores), `description` (tells Claude when and why to use it), and `input_schema` (JSON Schema for arguments). The description is critical — Claude reads it to decide when to call the tool. Be specific about what the tool does, what it returns, and when it should be used. Use `strict: true` for guaranteed schema conformance.

sections.title: The agentic loop
sections.body: The tool use flow is a loop: send messages → check stop_reason → if 'tool_use', execute the tool and send results back → repeat until 'end_turn'. Each `tool_use` block has an `id` that must be matched in the corresponding `tool_result`. Multiple tools can be called in a single turn — handle them all before continuing. See [[anthropic/agent-sdk]] for a higher-level abstraction.

sections.title: Controlling tool usage
sections.body: The `tool_choice` parameter controls Claude's tool behavior: `auto` (default — Claude decides), `any` (must use at least one tool), `tool` with `name` (must use a specific tool), `none` (tools disabled). Use `any` when you know a tool call is needed. Use `tool` for structured extraction where you want a specific schema enforced.

sections.title: Server-side tools
sections.body: Anthropic provides built-in server-side tools: `web_search` (search the web, $10/1000 searches), `code_execution` (run Python code), `web_fetch` (fetch URLs, free), `bash` (run shell commands), and `text_editor` (edit files). These run on Anthropic infrastructure — no tool_result needed. Add them via `type` field instead of `input_schema`.
